title: SimiC-Suite
text:
Overview
SimiC-Suite is a software suite for phenotype-specific gene regulatory network (GRN) analysis from single-cell RNA-seq data. It brings together complementary tools for GRN inference, Regulon Activity Score (RAS) calculation, and downstream assessment and visualization.

title: SimiC-Suite
text:
Packages



Package
Language / ecosystem
Purpose




SimiCPipeline
Python
Phenotype-specific GRN inference and Regulon Activity Score (RAS) calculation.


SimiCviz
R / Bioconductor
Tool-agnostic assessment and visualization of GRN outputs.

title: SimiC-Suite
text:
SimiCPipeline
SimiCPipeline is the Python component of SimiC-Suite. It supports reproducible phenotype-specific GRN inference from single-cell RNA-seq data and calculation of Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values for downstream biological interpretation.
SimiCPipeline builds on the SimiC framework for phenotype-aware regulatory network analysis. Please cite the original SimiC work when using SimiCPipeline where appropriate.

title: SimiC-Suite
text:
SimiCviz
SimiCviz is the R / Bioconductor component of SimiC-Suite. It provides tool-agnostic workflows for assessing, comparing, and visualizing GRN outputs, supporting interpretation across GRN inference methods.

title: Documentation
text:
SimiCPipeline
Use SimiCPipeline when the goal is to infer phenotype-specific gene regulatory networks from single-cell RNA-seq data and compute Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values.
Main repository:
https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCPipeline

title: Documentation
text:
SimiCviz
Use SimiCviz when the goal is to assess, compare, and visualize GRN outputs in R / Bioconductor workflows. SimiCviz is designed to be tool-agnostic, so it can support outputs from different GRN inference approaches.
Main repository:
https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCviz

title: Documentation
text:
Suggested Workflow

Prepare single-cell RNA-seq data and phenotype annotations.
Run SimiCPipeline for phenotype-specific GRN inference.
Calculate Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values with SimiCPipeline.
Use SimiCviz to assess and visualize GRN outputs.
